description: Horror requires element configurations that sustain dread through the presence of a genuine threat, isolation or vulnerability of characters, and places that amplify fear.

character_constraints.description: A character the audience identifies with whose survival is in genuine doubt.
A threat entity — monster, killer, supernatural force, or systemic horror — that generates fear.
Supporting characters whose vulnerability or death raises stakes for the protagonist.
A character or sign that warns of the coming threat — often ignored or misunderstood.

relationship_constraints.description: The protagonist must be in direct opposition to the threat entity.
Bond between protagonist and supporting characters — makes their potential loss meaningful.

place_constraints.description: The threat's domain or hunting ground — a place where the antagonist has advantage.
A normal starting environment that contrasts with the horror, making violation more disturbing.
An apparent safe space that may be violated or revealed as false security.

object_constraints.description: A means of fighting the threat — often inadequate, emphasizing vulnerability.
An object connected to the origin of the horror — may provide clues to defeating it.

element_rules.description: The threat must be present or implied from early in the story and must escalate.
element_rules.testable_condition: The antagonist is referenced as participant or in transitions by the second timeline moment.

element_rules.description: At least one character must die or be irreversibly harmed to demonstrate the threat is real.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'dies' transition appears in the timeline for a non-antagonist character.

element_rules.description: The protagonist must be isolated or have their support systems stripped away progressively.
element_rules.testable_condition: The number of ally participants decreases across timeline moments, or 'loses' transitions reduce the protagonist's support.

element_rules.description: At least one place must function as a trap — characters cannot easily leave.
element_rules.testable_condition: A place of type 'stronghold' appears in at least 3 consecutive timeline moments.
